$comment: The app's SHAPE vocabulary — the companion to tokens.contract.json.
The token contract stopped the two surfaces' COLOURS from drifting apart. Nothing
stopped their SHAPES, and a design system draws them by hand, from reading this
source. It has now been wrong twice and the GUARD has been wrong twice, which is why
this file says so much about its own limits.
`evidence` — the FULL class attribute of the element carrying the shape. It must be
present, verbatim, in `source`, outside comments and outside <script>. Checked
HERE, on the commit that changes the component.
WHAT THIS IS: a check that the element is still CLASSED the way it was read.
WHAT IT IS NOT: a check that it still LOOKS that way. A stylesheet can restyle it
in place without touching a class — so app.css is separately forbidden from
targeting any class this contract names, and `0.5px` (the marketing site's
hairline, which this package does not use and the design system says it does not)
is banned as a CSS value outright. Those two rules close the paths that exist;
they do not make the evidence check into something it is not.
`preview` — what the design system's product page must DRAW, and `sample`, the
element it draws it on. The far end RENDERS that page in a browser and reads
getComputedStyle off the real element. It does not parse CSS.
That is the second lesson, and it cost two rounds. The first version compared
substrings; the second modelled the cascade in JavaScript. Both were walked
around — by a longhand after a shorthand (`border-bottom` after `border`), by a
higher-specificity selector, by `!important`, by an inline style. A model of the
pixel is not the pixel. Ask the browser.
It names no other repository and no private path: it is a list of class names checked
against this package's own components.

chat-user.what: The user's turn is a bubble. It is the only thing in the chat that is.
evidence: class="rounded-[var(--radius-md)] px-4 py-2.5 text-sm max-w-[80%] text-left cursor-pointer hover:opacity-80 transition-opacity {msg.failed ? 'bg-danger/10 border border-danger/30 text-danger' : msg.queued ? 'bg-bg-muted border border-border text-text-muted' : 'bg-accent/10 border border-accent/20'}"

chat-assistant.what: The assistant's turn has no container at all — but it carries the AI-disclosure badge, which is not optional (EU AI Act Art. 50(1)).
evidence: class="ai-badge float-left mr-2 mt-[3px] inline-flex items-center rounded-[var(--radius-sm)] border border-accent/40 bg-accent/10 px-1.5 py-0.5 text-[10px] font-mono uppercase tracking-widest text-accent-text"

chat-tool.what: A tool call is a left rule, never a card — but once it settles it earns a full-strength tick.
evidence: class="flex items-center gap-2 md:gap-1.5 text-[13px] md:text-[11px] text-text-subtle/70 border-l-2 {isError ? 'border-danger/40' : isRunning ? 'border-warning/40' : 'border-border'} pl-3 py-1 md:py-0.5"

chat-tool-tick.what: The tick is full strength — the rule is for glancing, the tick is for when you actually look.

inbox-list.what: A SPACED list — 2px apart (space-y-0.5). Not hairline-separated rows. The `display` is part of the shape: set it to block and the gap goes inert while still computing to 2px.

inbox-row.what: A bordered card — --radius-sm, 1px border, transparent at rest so nothing shifts when it lights up.
evidence: class="rounded-[var(--radius-sm)] border transition-colors {isActiveSelection ? 'border-accent bg-accent/5' : 'border-transparent hover:border-border hover:bg-bg-subtle/60'}"

inbox-row-selected.what: Selection is the loudest thing on the screen: the accent at full strength, over a 5% wash.

inbox-bar.what: The toolbar sits on a 40% wash of the subtle ground — enough to separate, not enough to announce itself.
evidence: class="border-b border-border bg-bg-subtle/40 px-4 py-2 flex items-center gap-2"

state.what: The DOMINANT state surface: a tint, a border of the SAME colour at low opacity, and the text in the semantic colour. (A minority of surfaces — five, all warning — use a full-strength border instead; see `stateExceptions`.)
evidence: class="rounded-[var(--radius-md)] bg-danger/10 border border-danger/20 px-4 py-3 text-sm text-danger mb-4"

checkbox.what: The one real primitive. 16px, and it keeps a visible focus ring — in the accent-TEXT role, because the accent fill is too dark to read as an outline on the dark ground.

$comment: The design system says the app's state surfaces are a tint + a same-colour border at
LOW opacity + the text in the semantic colour. That is the DOMINANT pattern, not a
universal, and three separate attempts to state it as one were wrong in three
different directions. So the exceptions are counted, and the count is checked.
RESTING borders only. The previous regex had no left boundary and counted
`hover:border-danger` as a full-strength border, which made the prose claim a
destructive button carried one when it does not until you point at it.
Of the 7: two danger — a count badge and one destructive button, NEITHER a state
surface — and five warning, which are. Success never does it.
Facts the design system states in prose. Prose rots silently; these do not.
Scope is the LIBRARY — src/lib/components + src/lib/primitives, i.e. what pro imports.
The standalone app's routes/ are not part of it.
scopedStyle is split because the design system said 'six COMPONENTS carry scoped CSS'
and the guard counted components + primitives in one bucket, so it could never fail on
the sentence it existed to protect. It is five components and one primitive.
